description: ADSC internal safety/ethics policy rules (spec v4.2, D9/D11/R13). These are project policy, not law.

rules.title: No unconsented active interference with a registered space object (ADSC policy)
rules.source_reference: ADSC specification v4.2, decisions D9 and D11 (adsc-specification-v4.md)
rules.finding_if_fail: ADSC policy blocks any active interference or proximity operation without an affirmative owner-consent declaration. Absence of an affirmative 'true' is treated as no consent.
rules.limitations: Internal policy, stricter than a legal minimum by design. For research profiles the consent flag is a declared scenario assumption (D9).

rules.title: No live-ephemeris ingestion and no target-specific operational products (ADSC policy)
rules.source_reference: ADSC specification v4.2, decision D11 and hard rule R13 (adsc-specification-v4.md)
rules.finding_if_fail: The profile ingests live ephemerides/TLEs and/or generates target-specific operational products. ADSC policy (D11/R13) blocks both: targets stay class-level parameters and no operational approach products are produced.
rules.limitations: Internal policy. Either flag alone fails the rule; a missing flag evaluates UNKNOWN, never PASS.

rules.title: Non-weaponization: research-only or consented, class-level use (ADSC policy)
rules.source_reference: ADSC specification v4.2, section 1 and decision D11; README peaceful-use disclaimer
rules.finding_if_fail: The profile is neither research-only nor a consented, class-level use. ADSC deliverables must not support unconsented or weaponizable applications.
rules.limitations: Proxy check: 'non-weaponization' is not directly observable from profile fields; the rule uses research-only / consent / no-targeting-products as the observable proxy. Internal policy, not law.

rules.title: Export-control review status (ITAR/EAR precheck placeholder)
rules.authority: ADSC project policy (referencing US ITAR/EAR as the typical regime)
rules.source_date_or_version: snapshot 2024-era regime structure; regimes change - re-verify
rules.finding_if_fail: Export-control review is not complete. Open-literature methods (spec D11) are the current basis for research distribution, but a documented review is required before sharing controlled technical data or starting hardware/flight work.
rules.limitations: Placeholder: jurisdiction-specific export regimes (US ITAR/EAR cited as the typical case) are not modelled in detail. Requires export-control counsel review.

rules.title: WP5 campaign compliance metadata consistent with this mission profile
rules.source_reference: generated/wp5_campaign_runs.csv compliance columns (schema 1.0); ADSC specification v4.2, D9/D11
rules.detail_on_pass: WP5 campaign metadata consistent with research profile
rules.finding_if_fail: The committed WP5 campaign metadata columns disagree with this mission profile - the research-evidence chain is inconsistent and must be reconciled.
rules.limitations: Consistency check between two research artifacts; it makes no statement about the real world. If the WP5 CSV is absent the result is UNKNOWN, never PASS.
